context: Tests whether the agent writes alerting rules with for clauses, replaces increase() with rate() in alert expressions, and implements a multi-window burn-rate alert using both a short and long window combined with 'and'.

checklist.name: for clause on all alerts
checklist.description: Every alert rule in the file includes a 'for:' clause — no alert rule is missing it

checklist.name: rate() not increase() in alert expr
checklist.description: The corrected high-error alert uses rate() in its expr, not increase()

checklist.name: Multi-window alert present
checklist.description: An alert rule uses 'and' to combine two separate window conditions (one long window e.g. 1h, one short window e.g. 5m)

checklist.name: Burn rate multiplier applied
checklist.description: The burn-rate alert expression includes a burn rate factor (e.g., 14.4 or similar multiplier) relative to the error budget

checklist.name: job label filter present
checklist.description: Alert expressions include a job label filter (e.g., {job="checkout"})

checklist.name: rate() per-second threshold in corrected alert
checklist.description: The corrected alert threshold is expressed as a per-second rate value (a decimal < 1) rather than a raw event count

checklist.name: by() on aggregations
checklist.description: Any sum() in alert expressions includes an explicit by() or without() clause

checklist.name: Valid YAML alert rule format
checklist.description: The file uses valid Prometheus alerting rule YAML structure with 'alert:', 'expr:', and 'for:' keys
